protocols.name: Mediterranean
protocols.description: Plant-forward pattern emphasising olive oil, vegetables, legumes, whole grains, fish, and moderate wine.

protocols.name: DASH
protocols.description: Dietary Approaches to Stop Hypertension: high in fruits, vegetables, whole grains, low-fat dairy, lean protein; low in sodium, added sugars, and saturated fat.

protocols.name: low-FODMAP
protocols.description: Three-phase elimination diet that restricts fermentable oligo-, di-, monosaccharides, and polyols, then systematically reintroduces them under dietitian supervision.
core_components: Phase 2: structured reintroduction (one FODMAP at a time)
Avoid: onion, garlic, wheat, lactose, apples, pears, honey, polyols
Allow: lactose-free dairy, GF grains, low-FODMAP fruit/veg, lean protein

protocols.name: renal-friendly
protocols.description: Restricted-protein, low-sodium, controlled-potassium and -phosphorus pattern for non-dialysis CKD stages 3-5.

protocols.name: gluten-free
protocols.description: Strict elimination of gluten-containing grains (wheat, barley, rye) with attention to fibre, iron, folate, calcium, and vitamin D adequacy.
core_components: Eliminate wheat, barley, rye, contaminated oats
Use rice, quinoa, buckwheat, certified GF oats, corn
Read labels for hidden gluten (sauces, processed foods)
Supplement fibre, iron, folate, calcium, vitamin D as needed

protocols.name: low-sodium
protocols.description: Pattern restricting sodium to <2000-2300 mg/day for blood pressure, heart failure, and CKD management.
core_components: Cook from scratch using fresh herbs/spices

protocols.name: low-glycaemic
protocols.description: Carbohydrate-quality focused pattern emphasising low-GI foods to improve post-prandial glycaemia.
core_components: Choose low-GI carbohydrates (legumes, intact whole grains, non-starchy veg)
Pair carbohydrates with protein/fat to blunt response
Limit refined grains and sugar-sweetened beverages
Include vinegar/acidic foods to lower meal GI

protocols.name: ketogenic
protocols.description: Very-low-carbohydrate, high-fat ketogenic pattern (medical, not lifestyle) used under specialist supervision for refractory epilepsy and select metabolic indications.
core_components: Fat 70-80% kcal (4:1 classic ratio for epilepsy)
Protein adequate to prevent loss (1.0-1.5 g/kg)

protocols.name: MIND
protocols.description: Mediterranean-DASH Intervention for Neurodegenerative Delay: hybrid of Mediterranean and DASH with emphasis on berries, leafy greens, nuts, and olive oil for cognitive protection.
core_components: Limit red meat, butter, cheese, sweets, fried food

protocols.name: plant-based
protocols.description: Whole-food plant-based pattern centred on vegetables, fruits, legumes, whole grains, nuts, and seeds; may be vegan or near-vegan.
core_components: Vegetables and fruits at every meal
Plan iron, zinc, omega-3, calcium, vitamin D, iodine sources
protocols.source: AND Position on Vegetarian Diets 2016; AHA Scientific Statement 2023
